title: Sonanadi Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Sonanadi Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Uttarakhand

overview.short: Sonanadi Wildlife Sanctuary is a protected area in the Pauri Garhwal district of Uttarakhand, India.
overview.description: Sonanadi Wildlife Sanctuary is a protected area in the Pauri Garhwal district of Uttarakhand, India. Established in 1987, it encompasses an area of 301.18 km² and serves as a vital component of the Corbett Tiger Reserve. The sanctuary is named after the Sonanadi River (meaning "River of Gold"), which flows through the sanctuary.

topPlaces.name: Amangarh Tiger Reserve
topPlaces.description: New Jim Corbett Park also known as Amangarh Tiger Reserve, is a Tiger Reserve under Project Tiger in India. It is in Amangarh in Bijnor district in the state of Uttar Pradesh.

items.note: New Jim Corbett Park also known as Amangarh Tiger Reserve, is a Tiger Reserve under Project Tiger in India. It is in Amangarh in Bijnor district in the state of Uttar Pradesh.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Sonanadi Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Sonanadi Wildlife Sanctuary is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Sonanadi Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Dehradun Airport (Jolly Grant) (75 km) and the nearest railway station is Haridwar Junction (58 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Sonanadi Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Sonanadi Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Sonanadi Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Sonanadi Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Sonanadi Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Amangarh Tiger Reserve.
